articles.title: THE WORLD'S BIGGEST TECH STAGE WAITS FOR YOU
articles.summary: VivaTech is Europe's largest startup and tech event, with over 180,000 ATTENDEES FROM 170+ COUNTRIES. It's where global business gets done and industries are reshaped. This year, VivaTech hosts its exclusive CMO Summit [5], powered by Adobe and The New York Times. Built by and for marketing leaders:

articles.title: HOW META'S AI PUSH IS CHANGING AD CREATION
articles.summary: Meta is pushing toward fully automated advertising, with a reported goal of letting businesses simply input a credit card and a goal by the end of 2026. In practice, media buyers say the platform is far from ready. The new Andromeda ad retrieval system incentivizes uploading diverse creative to broad audiences rather than targeting fixed segments, which has dramatically increased production demands. Advantage+ campaigns now account for 60-70% of spending at some agencies, but recurring issues with low-quality placements and overspend in weak demographics mean significant human oversight is still necessary. Most large brands remain unwilling to use Meta's generative AI for ad creative due to legal concerns and brand control.

articles.title: GEN Z-ERS ARE BECOMING BOSSES
articles.summary: Gen Z is reshaping workplace norms. They reject the old model of loyalty and delayed rewards. Raised with constant access to information, they want clear reasoning behind decisions and faster trust. This clashes with older leadership styles built on hierarchy and slow-earned credibility. Companies that flatten structures and involve younger employees will retain talent.

articles.title: WIN REPORT: ONE WORD, 38% MORE BOOKINGS
articles.summary: Adding one word to a call-to-action drove large gains because it closed the intention-action gap. Changing a search button from “Search” to “Search Now” increased bookings by 38% on a travel marketplace. Many visitors were browsing and delaying action due to choice paralysis and present bias. The word “Now” worked because searching is low risk and requires no commitment. It also added urgency and gave a clear instruction, which reduced hesitation and pushed users to act immediately.

articles.title: THE POWER OF A FLYWHEEL
articles.summary: Growth accelerates when channels reinforce each other instead of operating alone. LinkedIn drives visibility and starts conversations, while YouTube builds trust and captures high-intent search. The newsletter deepens relationships and converts more effectively, since it reaches an owned audience. Content is repurposed across all three so each idea compounds and stays active longer. This creates a flywheel where every piece of content feeds the next, driving consistent leads without ads or outreach.

articles.title: THE CLAUDE DTC SECOND BRAIN
articles.summary: This guide shows how to turn scattered business data into a self-improving AI knowledge base using Claude. Instead of manually organizing files, users dump raw inputs like ads, reviews, and notes into folders. Then the AI compiles them into a structured, cross-linked wiki that answers strategic questions using only that data. The system continuously improves by saving outputs and re-ingesting them, creating a feedback loop where insights compound over time. It demonstrates a simple but powerful shift from fragmented tools to a single AI-maintained “brand brain” that makes strategy grounded in real data.

articles.title: GOOGLE QUIETLY LAUNCHED AN AI DICTATION APP THAT WORKS OFFLINE
articles.summary: Google AI Edge Eloquent is a free iOS dictation app that turns speech into polished text using on-device AI. It transcribes in real time, removes filler words, and can rewrite output into summaries or more formal versions. Users can choose local-only or cloud processing, add custom terms, and search past transcripts while tracking stats like word count and speaking speed. It may expand to Android in the near future.

articles.title: A MASSIVE RANT ABOUT USING LLMS FOR MARKETING TASKS
articles.summary: This article catalogues specific examples of LLMs failing at tasks they are commonly promoted for, including company research, data analysis, transcription, and file exports. One model fabricated 30 businesses during a prospecting task, then claimed it had verified them via live web searches it never performed. Another task involving Search Console data produced invented page names and inconsistent metrics despite having the actual export attached. The real danger is not just the occasional errors but confident, repeated fabrication paired with corporate decisions to replace junior marketers with AI tools.

articles.title: WHY SAAS GOT PRICED OUT
articles.summary: Public SaaS valuations collapsed despite continued growth, which shows that the market no longer believes in the traditional model. Median SaaS multiples fell from 18.6× revenue in 2021 to 5.1× in 2025, even as HubSpot grew revenue 141% while its stock fell 71%. The core issue is the input model, since software built on manual data entry is being replaced by AI that understands unstructured input like voice and context. Speed gains from AI are quickly commoditized, while quality problems like model collapse and error scaling make judgment more valuable than output. Future products will win by integrating into AI-driven workflows and improving quality. Users now expect tools to work with how they already use AI rather than forcing new systems.

articles.title: COULD GAMING'S 'EPIC' STRUGGLES OPEN THE DOOR FOR MORE ADVERTISERS?
articles.summary: Epic Games' recent layoff of a thousand employees and Fortnite's declining audience highlight a wider downturn in gaming, but industry insiders say advertising alone would not have solved Epic's core challenges around engagement and mobile access. Large publishers remain reluctant to adopt scalable ad formats, often stuck in a cycle of licensing brand IP and then awkwardly asking those same brands to buy ad placements. Roblox is the furthest along in testing in-game ads, but revenue from advertising is still modest, and many of its game developers do not see the value in running them.

articles.title: WHY THE AI MOAT CONVERSATION IS MISSING ITS MOST IMPORTANT LAYER
articles.summary: The real moat is becoming “hard to believe”, meaning earning trust and credibility over time.

articles.title: INSTAGRAM CHIEF DEBUNKS POPULAR ENGAGEMENT HACK
articles.summary: Re-posting feed posts to Instagram Stories doesn't boost reach.
